summary: Scope refined without changing CORS semantics: generated artifacts were removed from diff, only target product files remain, and verification is now reported with one factual status per command.
what_changed: Cleaned generated artifacts from git changes (`rtb/**/__pycache__/*.pyc`) after initial inspection and again after test runs regenerated them.
Did not modify product code beyond existing CORS/preflight implementation; final changed set is still only the 3 target files.
Re-verified CORS centralization and no route-level CORS hacks: policy remains in constants and middleware wiring in main app.
Ran full required verification command list and captured exact outcomes (including environment-caused failures).
risks: Backend pytest-based commands currently fail in this environment due filesystem permission denial creating `.pytest-temp-tests/*` (WinError 5), which blocks test execution before CORS assertions run.
Because of that environment blocker, required pytest command status is failing even though direct bounded preflight probe succeeds.
notes: `git diff --stat` after cleanup: 3 files changed, 50 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-).
`__pycache__` / `.pyc` are no longer present in diff (confirmed by `git status --short` and pattern check).
`export_project_to_txt.py` sync: not needed for this scope; not run and not edited.
verification_notes: 1) `python -c "from rtb.app.main import app; print(app.title)"` -> exit 0, output `MetaPlatform Runtime Backend`.

2) `python -m pytest rtb/tests/test_imports.py -k cors_preflight -q` -> exit 1, failed at fixture setup in `rtb/tests/conftest.py:20` with `PermissionError [WinError 5]` creating `.pytest-temp-tests/...` (environment issue).
3) `cd ui && npm.cmd test` -> exit 0, all UI tests passed (`pass 220`, `fail 0`).
4) `cd ui && npm.cmd run test:integration` -> exit 0, delegates to `npm test`, passed (`pass 220`, `fail 0`).
5) `cd ui && npm.cmd run test:backend` -> exit 1, pytest errors (67) with same `PermissionError [WinError 5]` on `.pytest-temp-tests/...` during fixture setup (environment issue).
6) `cd ui && npm.cmd run test:all` -> exit 1; `npm test` phase passed, backend phase failed with same `PermissionError [WinError 5]` (environment issue).
7) Diff verification: final `git diff --stat` shows only the 3 target files; no generated artifacts remain changed.

8) Additional bounded check (supplemental): direct TestClient OPTIONS probes for `/api/commands`, `/api/project-imports`, `/api/project-exports/preflight-probe` all returned `200`, `access-control-allow-origin=http://127.0.0.1:5173`, credentials `true`, and methods/headers from centralized policy.
